title: Labor-Market Research, Role Families, and Data Limits · Career Evidence Case: Role Hypothesis
summary: A candidate follows viral title and salary lists that mix countries, seniority, occupations, posting windows, and unsupported predictions. Predict the hiring decision, evidence quality, affected people, uncertainty, and ethical boundary before reviewing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the role hypothesis increment in dated labor-market evidence brief. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select a small, testable role-family hypothesis from personal constraints, evidence adjacency, real tasks, and market signals.
Compare occupation and location wage distributions while accounting for level, total compensation, hours, contract status, and data lag.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Predict Role Hypothesis · Theory Role Hypothesis
steps.instruction:
theory case — A viral salary post combines national medians, remote listings, senior titles, old data, and one technology trend into a guaranteed entry-level forecast. The theory variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Select a small, testable role-family hypothesis from personal constraints, evidence adjacency, real tasks, and market signals.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Role Hypothesis contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Role Hypothesis · Theory Role Hypothesis.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for select a small, testable role-family hypothesis from personal constraints, evidence adjacency, real tasks, and market signals. For Predict Role Hypothesis · Theory Role Hypothesis, keep Role Hypothesis connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.text: A viral salary post combines national medians, remote listings, senior titles, old data, and one technology trend into a guaranteed entry-level forecast. content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A viral salary post combines national medians, remote listings, senior titles, old data, and one technology trend into a guaranteed entry-level forecast. The worked example variation 4 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Select a small, testable role-family hypothesis from personal constraints, evidence adjacency, real tasks, and market signals. A defensible solution applies Role Hypothesis, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to dated labor-market evidence brief.

content.code:
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-candidate-3
target-role: accessible software quality engineer
jurisdiction: example location requiring current official review
audience: technical reviewer with ten minutes and keyboard-only access
claim: one bounded role behavior is supported by a revision-linked changed case
evidence: repository case study, exact test result, contribution note, and limitation
process-state: investigate before applying
accessibility: structured text, reflow, visible focus, plain-language status, and equivalent format
privacy-consent: publish no sensitive person or third-party data; contact only with explicit consent
decision: proceed only after identity, role, evidence, access, and risk checks pass
verify: inspect links, extraction order, revision, changed case, disclosure, and source date
failure: reject invented impact, hidden assistance, exposed data, or unverified recruiter identity
repair: correct the first unsupported claim and add a changed-candidate regression
owner: learner controls every external action
transfer-boundary: manual learner-approved profile, application, conversation, interview, and offer decisions
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Keeping every possible role open always increases opportunity. This fails the Role Hypothesis contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: GitHub Healthy Contributions Guidance (GitHub Docs current 2026-07-15) — Controls README, contribution, conduct, license, support, governance, issue-template, and stewardship signals without equating activity volume with quality.

content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select a small, testable role-family hypothesis from personal constraints, evidence adjacency, real tasks, and market signals.
